description: The editor has two modes when a block is selected: text mode (you're editing inside the block) and block mode (the whole block is selected as a unit).

parent.description: This guide is for content editors using a Hydra-powered site. It covers how to use the editor — how to select things, edit text, add and move blocks, work with containers and templates — without assuming you know how the site was built.
parent.title: Editor Guide

title: Selecting blocks

p-1.plaintext: The editor has two modes when a block is selected: **text mode** (you're editing inside the block) and **block mode** (the whole block is selected as a unit).

children.text: The editor has two modes when a block is selected:
(the whole block is selected as a unit).

p-3.plaintext: Click on a block in the preview. If the frontend marks any of its fields as inline-editable, the cursor goes into that field and you can start typing.

children.text: Click on a block in the preview. If the frontend marks any of its fields as inline-editable, the cursor goes into that field and you can start typing.

ul-4.plaintext: A subtle border appears around the block. The field you're editing gets a faint underline. The Quanta toolbar appears above the block (formatting, convert-to, delete, etc.). The sidebar switches to that block's settings.

children.text: A subtle border appears around the block.
The field you're editing gets a faint underline.
The Quanta toolbar appears above the block (formatting, convert-to, delete, etc.).
The sidebar switches to that block's settings.

img-5.alt: Slate paragraph in text mode — cursor in the field, sidebar showing block's settings.

p-7.plaintext: Press `Escape` to leave text editing. The block stays selected, but you're no longer inside any specific field.

children.text: to leave text editing. The block stays selected, but you're no longer inside any specific field.

ul-8.plaintext: A full border appears around the block (visually stronger than the text-mode hint). Keyboard shortcuts now operate on the whole block:

children.text: A full border appears around the block (visually stronger than the text-mode hint).
Keyboard shortcuts now operate on the whole block:

p-9.plaintext: - **Arrow Up / Down** — move selection to the previous / next sibling block (container-aware: jumps into and out of containers).   - **Enter** — add a new block after this one.   - **Delete / Backspace** — remove the selected block.

children.text: — move selection to the previous / next sibling block (container-aware: jumps into and out of containers).   -
— add a new block after this one.   -

p-10.plaintext: Press `Escape` again to **deselect** (or go up to the parent container if this block is inside one). Each `Escape` walks one step up the hierarchy.

children.text: (or go up to the parent container if this block is inside one). Each
walks one step up the hierarchy.

img-11.alt: Same paragraph in block mode — full blue border, no cursor.

p-13.plaintext: You can select multiple blocks at once and operate on the group.

children.text: You can select multiple blocks at once and operate on the group.
Select range from currently-selected block to the clicked one
Toggle the clicked block in/out of the selection
Extend or shrink the selection by one block
Clears multi-selection, selects only the clicked block

ul-16.plaintext: A combined bounding box is drawn around them. Delete / Backspace removes all of them. The Quanta toolbar dropdown offers actions that apply to all (e.g. "Wrap in...", see Containers). The sidebar shows the count and lists each selected block by type.

children.text: A combined bounding box is drawn around them.
The Quanta toolbar dropdown offers actions that apply to all (e.g. "Wrap in...", see
The sidebar shows the count and lists each selected block by type.

img-17.alt: Two adjacent paragraphs multi-selected — combined bounding box, sidebar shows "2 selected", toolbar shows count badge.

p-19.plaintext: The sidebar is the other way to navigate selection — useful when the block you want is offscreen, buried inside a paged container (e.g. a specific slide of a slider), or you just prefer keyboard / pointer nav over hunting in the preview.

children.text: The sidebar is the other way to navigate selection — useful when the block you want is offscreen, buried inside a paged container (e.g. a specific slide of a slider), or you just prefer keyboard / pointer nav over hunting in the preview.

p-21.plaintext: When a block is selected, the sidebar shows the **chain of parent containers** from the root down to the block — one collapsible section per level. Each level has a `‹` arrow on the left.

children.text: When a block is selected, the sidebar shows the
from the root down to the block — one collapsible section per level. Each level has a

tabs.label: Text
tabs.code:
‹ Columns          ← click ‹ to deselect (go to page level)
   [Columns settings]
   ‹ Column        ← click ‹ to select Columns
      [Column settings]
      ‹ Text       ← current block, highlighted
         [Text body, …]

p-23.plaintext: Click any `‹` arrow to **navigate up** to that level. This does the same thing as pressing `Escape` repeatedly, but visibly — you can see what each parent is named, jump several levels in one click, and edit the parent's own settings (alignment, padding, …) inline without leaving the current selection.

children.text: to that level. This does the same thing as pressing

repeatedly, but visibly — you can see what each parent is named, jump several levels in one click, and edit the parent's own settings (alignment, padding, …) inline without leaving the current selection.

img-24.alt: Sidebar showing parent chain for a slate inside a column inside columns. Three sections each with a `‹` arrow: Columns ("My Columns Section"), Column ("Left Column"), Text (current, body field shown).

p-25.plaintext: This works for any depth — nested columns, slider with templated children, accordion inside a section inside the page. The chain reflects the real DOM hierarchy.

children.text: This works for any depth — nested columns, slider with templated children, accordion inside a section inside the page. The chain reflects the real DOM hierarchy.

p-27.plaintext: When a container block is selected, the sidebar shows that container's **children** as a list, one row per child:

children.text: When a container block is selected, the sidebar shows that container's
as a list, one row per child:

ul-29.plaintext: ⋮⋮ drag handle — drag to reorder children within the container. > drill-in arrow — selects that child, scrolls the preview to it, switches the sidebar to its settings. [+] add button — opens the BlockChooser to add a new child to that field.

children.text: — drag to reorder children within the container.
— selects that child, scrolls the preview to it, switches the sidebar to its settings.
— opens the BlockChooser to add a new child to that field.

p-30.plaintext: If the container has multiple blocks fields (e.g. a header field and a body field), each appears as a separate section with its own children list and add button.

children.text: If the container has multiple blocks fields (e.g. a header field and a body field), each appears as a separate section with its own children list and add button.

img-31.alt: Sidebar with a columns container selected, showing two children-list sections — Top Images (with two Image rows) and Columns (with two Column rows). Each row has a `⋮⋮` drag handle and a `>` drill-in arrow; each section has a `+` add button.

h-32.plaintext: Picking from the outline (for paged containers)

children.text: Picking from the outline (for paged containers)

p-33.plaintext: Some containers paginate their children — a slider only renders the active slide; an accordion only the expanded panel. Use the children list to pick a slide / panel that's not currently visible — the preview scrolls and pages to it automatically.

children.text: Some containers paginate their children — a slider only renders the active slide; an accordion only the expanded panel. Use the children list to pick a slide / panel that's not currently visible — the preview scrolls and pages to it automatically.

p-35.plaintext: The page itself has metadata fields (title, description, preview image). Some are inline-editable in the preview (e.g. clicking the rendered `<h1>` to edit the page title); all are editable from the sidebar when no block is selected.

children.text: The page itself has metadata fields (title, description, preview image). Some are inline-editable in the preview (e.g. clicking the rendered
to edit the page title); all are editable from the sidebar when no block is selected.
